approach: The CR moves two shipped #9 suggestions off stderr onto stdout, per the axi node's corrected contextual-disclosure principle. It is the fourth mission in this lineage and the first to BUILD the help[N] block the corrected #9 describes but no frozen suite yet pins, so this CR sets that block's concrete shape.

Two paths, NOT symmetric, and the originating follow-up (#36 ledger seq 6) got one wrong. Path 1 is layout save's truncation note (noteTabsLeftOut, cli.ts:587), genuinely pinned by frozen layout.feature 'a bare save says what it left out' AND colliding with frozen 'save writes the path and nothing else' — the note's stderr home was BOUGHT to keep stdout a bare path for $(...) composition, so moving it is a real design tension, not a stream flip. Path 2 is the grouping hint (reportOpenedWorktree, cli.ts:252): the issue calls it 'open' but it is worktree add/open, pinned only by the stream-AGNOSTIC mux.feature:1238 'the caller is told the placement cost the grouping' — so moving it to a stdout help[] field is ADDITIVE and needs no re-open. The follow-up's 'needs a cleared re-open of the layout suite' is wrong for path 2.

Clearance is expected to fire for path 1 (a frozen layout scenario is rewritten) and must be RATIFIED in-session before any frozen scenario is narrowed. The composition-vs-#9 resolution for save is a genuine judgment the conductor is grilling the requester on up front. Compatibility: package is 0.0.0, nothing shipped, so a stream change breaks no caller's ceiling — checked, does not fire.
Leash auto-spec, derived: self-assert within the spec leash on non-narrowing work; STOP for ratification on the path-1 frozen re-open. Changeset owed at handoff for a user-facing CLI behavior change.
